name: aligned_zero_size
reason: aligned with size=0 should not be satisfiable (division by zero), indicating a missing precondition

name: upper_vaddr_no_progress
code:
proof fn phi_2_upper_vaddr_no_progress(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.upper_vaddr(layer, base) <= base,
{
}
reason: upper_vaddr should always exceed base under inv(), otherwise the layer maps a zero-size address range

name: entry_base_non_monotonic
code:
proof fn phi_3_entry_base_non_monotonic(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, idx: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        idx < arch.num_entries(layer),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, idx + 1) <= arch.entry_base(layer, base, idx),
{
}
reason: entry_base must be strictly monotonic in idx; non-monotonicity would cause overlapping page table entries

name: inv_allows_empty_arch
code:
proof fn phi_4_inv_allows_empty_arch(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
    ensures
        arch.layers.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: inv() should require at least one layer; an empty architecture cannot perform any address translation

name: deeper_layer_larger_entries
code:
proof fn phi_5_deeper_layer_larger_entries(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i + 1 < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size((i + 1) as nat) > arch.entry_size(i),
{
}
reason: in a valid page table hierarchy, deeper layers must have smaller-or-equal entry sizes; larger would invert the address decomposition
